title: Alsodeia lanceolata

The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Alsodeia lanceolata include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Alsodeia is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in the form of capsules, tablets, and ointments for its medicinal applications.
